schema.title: deployment / sizing topic
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: RAID layout for IoTDB data disks (multi-volume parallel write)
tuples.symptom: Sustained disk IO bottleneck (Disk Utilization % at 100% on single volume); compaction lag; write latency rises with throughput
tuples.root_cause: Single RAID group concentrates IoTDB writes on one logical device; cannot parallelize across spindles; one disk failure blocks all I/O
fix: Avoid single-disk-array for all disks — splits IoTDB's IO across too few volumes
Recommended: split into 1-6 disk groups for IoTDB data (each as separate volume /data1, /data2, ...)
Use RAID1 for system disk (2 disks); RAID5 in groups of 4-7 for data disks
Never use NAS / network storage — high latency under random IO
Configure IoTDB dn_data_dirs to point to the multiple volumes

tuples.title: Disable firewall + ensure cluster ports unblocked
tuples.symptom: DataNode cannot join cluster; consensus group keeps timing out; replication latency huge; election storm on Ratis
tuples.root_cause: firewalld blocks inter-node ports; missing iptables exception; required ports 10710/10720 (ConfigNode), 6667/10730/10740/10750/10760/9090/9190/3000 (DataNode) blocked
fix: systemctl stop firewalld && systemctl disable firewalld
Or selectively open ports via iptables/firewalld rules
Verify with: lsof -i:<port>  or  netstat -tunp | grep <port>
After unblock, check election: SHOW CONFIGNODES (Leader role assigned)

tuples.title: JDK 17 recommended for V1.3.2.2+ (older JDK has perf regressions)
tuples.symptom: DataNode cannot stop cleanly (process hangs on shutdown); periodic perf drop on older JDK; GC pauses worse than expected
tuples.root_cause: Some performance code paths (string interning, NIO file mappings) regressed on JDK 8/11 under IoTDB workload; JDK 17 has the fixes
fix: Install JDK 17, set JAVA_HOME and PATH
Verify with: java -version (expect 17.x)
Update IOTDB_HOME/conf/datanode-env.sh JAVA_HOME if pinned

tuples.title: Sizing CPU by number of timeseries (frequency ≤ 1 Hz)
tuples.symptom: Sustained CPU saturation that doesn't drop after KILL QUERY; can't keep up with insert rate; chronic compaction lag
tuples.root_cause: Under-provisioned CPU vs timeseries count; insert/query/compaction threads compete; throughput capped by CPU
fix: Recommended CPU per node (1Hz workload):
≤100K series → 2-4 cores
≤300K → 4-8 cores
≤500K → 8-26 cores
≤1M → 16-32 cores
≤2M → 32-48 cores
≤10M → 48+ cores; >10M consult Timecho

tuples.title: Sizing memory by number of timeseries
tuples.symptom: OOM during MemTable flush; chronic high GC time%; queries fail with 'not enough memory'
tuples.root_cause: Under-provisioned heap vs metadata + MemTable + cache + query temp; ConfigNode also holds partition table proportional to series count
fix: Recommended memory per node (1Hz workload):
≤100K series → 2-4 GB
Adjust IOTDB_HEAP_SIZE in datanode-env.sh accordingly

tuples.title: Sizing NIC bandwidth by write throughput
tuples.symptom: Network bandwidth saturation visible on Network dashboard; consensus replication lag; high tail latencies for writes
tuples.root_cause: 1Gbps NIC saturates ~125MB/s of payload; high-fanout writes (replication factor × points/sec × point size) easily exceed
fix: Write throughput < 10M points/s → 1 Gbps NIC OK
Write throughput ≥ 10M points/s → upgrade to 10 Gbps NIC
Estimate: throughput × point_size × replication_factor must fit < 0.7 × NIC bandwidth

tuples.title: Estimate disk space using IoTDB sizing formula
tuples.symptom: Disk fills faster than planned; SHOW DISK_USAGE shows surprising consumption per database
tuples.root_cause: Underestimating storage; compression ratio assumption (5-10x) varies with data type/encoding/uniqueness
fix: Formula: points × sampling_freq_Hz × point_bytes × storage_seconds × replicas ÷ compression_ratio
Point sizes: BOOLEAN=9B, INT32/FLOAT=12B, INT64/DOUBLE=16B, TEXT=8+avg(text_bytes)B
Use SSD for >10M timeseries or query-heavy workloads

tuples.title: System Dashboard — CPU panel: System vs Process CPU Utilization
tuples.symptom: Process CPU Utilization >80% sustained; System CPU much higher than Process CPU
tuples.root_cause: If Process CPU is high → IoTDB internal load (compaction/query/replication); if only System CPU is high → other tenants on host
fix: Process CPU high → check compaction queue & SHOW QUERIES for runaways
System CPU high (Process CPU low) → identify co-located workloads stealing CPU
Per-minute CPU Time helps confirm sustained vs spike load

tuples.title: System Dashboard — Disk panel: file count breakdown
tuples.symptom: TsFile / Seq / Unseq / WAL / Cross-Temp / Inner-Seq-Temp / Mods counts surging
tuples.root_cause: Different sub-counts hint at different bottlenecks: Cross-Temp/Inner-*-Temp climbing → compaction in progress but slow; Seq+Unseq climbing → compaction lag; WAL climbing → flush back-pressure
fix: Watch Cross-Temp and Inner-*-Temp to confirm compaction is running, not stuck
Mods accumulation → many deletions outpacing tombstone resolution
WAL count high + low Seq growth → MemTable flush stalled (heap pressure or fsync slow)

tuples.title: System Dashboard — Disk panel: Disk I/O Latency (Avg) + Queue Length
tuples.symptom: Disk I/O Latency (Avg) rises into hundreds of ms; Disk I/O Queue Length (Avg) consistently >1
tuples.root_cause: Disk saturated; either by IoTDB itself or by competing process; HDD vs SSD throughput limit reached
fix: If queue length >1 across multiple volumes → IoTDB IO bound; lower compaction throughput or add disks
If isolated to one volume → check dn_data_dirs allocation; rebalance
Consider migrating to SSD for query-heavy workloads

tuples.title: System Dashboard — JVM panel: GC Time Percentage + GC Events Breakdown
tuples.symptom: GC Time Percentage > 5% sustained; FGC (Full GC) frequency rising; GC Pause Time per minute > 1 second
tuples.root_cause: Heap too small or memory leak; high object allocation rate in MemTable / query / consensus log; concurrent collector falling behind
fix: If only YGC frequent: increase young generation size (-XX:NewSize) or reduce allocation pressure
If FGC frequent: increase IOTDB_HEAP_SIZE; consider switching to G1GC/ZGC (datanode-env.sh)
Heap dump on next FGC: -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError -XX:HeapDumpPath=...

tuples.title: System Dashboard — Network panel: Network Speed + Active TCP Connections
tuples.symptom: Network Speed near NIC rate; Active TCP Connections spiking abnormally
tuples.root_cause: Either replication payload exceeds NIC, or client connection storm (no pooling), or Ratis election retry loop
fix: If Network Speed near 1Gbps/10Gbps line: see DM-DR-3 (upgrade NIC) or reduce replication factor
If Active TCP Connections sky-high: enforce client connection pool; check for client reconnect loops
Cross-check Ratis logs for election retries — set heartbeat_interval_in_ms appropriately
